name: timemd-visualizor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Release 0.3.8

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Release 0.3.7

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Renames the plugin manifest to timemd-visualizor and includes the input tracking view.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Projects donut chart no longer renders overlapping arcs at the top** when the loaded reports have a "Top Apps" section truncated to a top-N subset (recent time.md exporter behavior). The plugin now reads the canonical total from the summary section and uses a denominator that can't be exceeded by category totals.
- TOTAL displayed in projects/today views is now accurate on truncated-app-list exports (previously undercounted).

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: \`bare: true\` now strips the inner card chrome too — \`.timemd-card\`, \`.timemd-stat\`, \`.timemd-stat-card\`, and the per-view stat tiles. Previously it only removed the outer embed wrapper, leaving smaller card tiles inside on multi-card embeds like \`overview\`, \`trends\`, and \`reports\`. Now every embed flattens cleanly onto the note background when bare is set.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Bare embeds

New \`bare: true\` param works on every embed. Strips the embed wrapper's background, border, and padding so the widget sits flush on the note background instead of inside a card.

Most useful for donut-only distribution embeds:

\`\`\`timemd
view: distribution
stats: false
legend: false
label: false
bare: true
\`\`\`

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Interactive distribution donut

The donut chart in the \`distribution\` embed is now hover-driven:
- Hovering a slice swaps the center text to that category's name, duration, and percentage
- Other slices fade out so the active one pops
- Matching legend items get an active highlight; siblings dim

## New params for donut-only widgets

Two new params let you strip the embed down to a chart-only widget where the hover IS the legend:

\`\`\`timemd
view: distribution
stats: false
legend: false
label: false
\`\`\`

| param | default | effect |
|-------|---------|--------|
| \`stats\` | \`true\` | \`false\` hides the STATS card |
| \`legend\` | \`true\` | \`false\` hides the category legend list |
| \`label\` | \`true\` | \`false\` hides the "DISTRIBUTION" header |

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Distribution embed

New standalone \`distribution\` embed — the donut chart, category legend, and stats card from the **Projects** view, available as a single widget you can drop into any note.

\`\`\`timemd
view: distribution
title: Distribution
\`\`\`

Pass \`stats: false\` to hide the STATS card for a pure chart + legend.

\`\`\`timemd
view: distribution
stats: false
\`\`\`

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## New views

Three new components mirroring the [time.md desktop app](https://timeprint.app):

- **Projects** — category groups with expand/collapse, donut distribution chart, and stats
- **Web History** — browser-filtered timeline, top domains, and hourly activity tabs
- **Reports** — time distribution and weekday averages charts, sortable report table, and CSV / JSON / Markdown clipboard export

Each is available as a full Obsidian view (command palette: *time.md: Open Projects*, *Open Web History*, *Open Reports*) and as an embeddable code block:

\`\`\`timemd
view: projects
\`\`\`

\`\`\`timemd
view: web-history
tab: domains
\`\`\`

\`\`\`timemd
view: reports
groupBy: category
\`\`\`

## New embed params

- \`tab\` — \`timeline\` | \`domains\` | \`activity\` (web-history)
- \`browser\` — filter web-history to a single browser
- \`groupBy\` — \`app\` | \`category\` | \`day\` (reports)
- \`format\` — \`csv\` | \`json\` | \`markdown\` (reports export)

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
The `view: overview` embed block now accepts a `date` parameter — `today`, `yesterday`, or `YYYY-MM-DD` — that re-derives every panel for that single day. Stats and the apps list are computed from the Raw Sessions section, the trend chart narrows to the matching day, and the heatmap becomes a 24-cell hourly strip. Falls back to a clear empty state if the export lacks Raw Sessions.

Example:

```timemd
view: overview
date: yesterday
title: Yesterday
```

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: The `view: overview` embed block now accepts a `sections` parameter so you can pick which panels render — any of `stats`, `trend`, `heatmap`, `apps` — alongside the existing `limit`, `days`, and `title` knobs. Omit it to keep the previous all-panels behavior.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Heatmap cells, month grid intensity, line charts, and bar fills can now be themed via two new color picker settings under Settings → time.md. Colors are exposed as CSS custom properties on `document.body` so changes take effect immediately on open views and embeds — no reload needed.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: The weekly heatmap now renders inside \`\`\`timemd view: overview\`\`\` code-block embeds, between the trend chart and the top apps bars. 0.2.5 only added it to the Overview tab — the embed has a separate render path.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Surface the weekly heatmap on the Overview view alongside Trend and Top apps, so weekday/hour intensity is visible without switching to the Calendar view.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Auto-reload views when the export folder changes.

- Subscribe to vault create/modify/delete/rename events and debounce a store reload (400ms) so new, edited, or renamed exports refresh all open views without tapping Reload.
- The manual Reload button, command, and settings toggle still work as escape hatches.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Guard against pathologically large export files.

- Skip files > 50 MB with a per-file reason (a 12-day export bloated to 855 MB due to an upstream table-padding bug froze Obsidian on read).
- Show skipped files in a red banner above every view so the user understands why data is missing.

Pairs with the time.md app fix that caps markdown table column widths at 200 chars.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Markdown table parser fixes for time.md combined exports.

- Handle unescaped `|` inside wiki-link cells. Previously the naive pipe split produced extra columns, so `total_seconds` ended up in the wrong field and totals were wildly wrong (user reported "30m" for a 7-hour day).
- Handle truncated wiki links where the exporter cuts cells mid-link and omits the closing `]]`.
- `stripWikiLinks` now extracts display text for both closed and unclosed links.

BRAT will auto-update on next Obsidian launch.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Overview/embed rendering fixes.

- Strip `[[wiki|links]]` unconditionally in markdown parser (frontmatter-less exports were rendering raw `[[Apps/com.foo.bar]]` text).
- Defensive wiki-link stripping in store accessors.
- Stat cards now truncate long values with ellipsis.
- Date range collapses to a single date when start == end.
- Single-point line chart now centers the dot instead of pinning it to the left edge.

BRAT will pick this up automatically.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## New: embed live widgets in your notes

Drop a \`timemd\` code block into any note:

\`\`\`timemd
view: overview
\`\`\`

### Supported views

| view | renders |
|------|---------|
| \`overview\` (default) | Stats strip + trend sparkline + top apps |
| \`stat\` | One big number (configure with \`metric:\`) |
| \`trends\` / \`trend-chart\` | Daily line chart |
| \`calendar\` / \`heatmap\` | 7×24 weekly heatmap |
| \`apps\` / \`top-apps\` | App bar list |
| \`categories\` | Category bar list |
| \`details\` | Recent sessions list |

### Parameters

- \`limit\` — number of items (overview/top-apps/categories/details)
- \`days\` — restrict trend to last N days
- \`metric\` — \`total_time\`, \`top_app\`, \`apps_count\`, \`days\`, \`peak_day\`
- \`title\` — optional heading

Widgets re-render automatically whenever you reload exports.

### Example — daily-note dashboard

\`\`\`timemd
view: stat
metric: total_time
title: Screen time today
\`\`\`

\`\`\`timemd
view: trend-chart
days: 7
\`\`\`

\`\`\`timemd
view: top-apps
limit: 5
\`\`\`

## Install via BRAT

If you're already on 0.1.0, BRAT will pick up 0.2.0 automatically. Otherwise: \`BRAT: Add a beta plugin\` → \`CodyBontecou/obsidian-timemd\`.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Initial release of the time.md plugin for Obsidian.

## Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from Obsidian's community plugins.
2. `BRAT: Add a beta plugin` → enter `CodyBontecou/obsidian-timemd`.
3. Enable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins.
4. Set **Settings → time.md → Export folder** to an in-vault folder containing your time.md exports.
5. Run `time.md: Open Overview` from the command palette.

## Supported import formats

Auto-detects JSON, CSV, Markdown, and Obsidian (YAML frontmatter + wiki links) exports from the time.md app.

## Views

- Overview — total time, top apps, trend sparkline
- Trends — daily line chart with period stats
- Calendar — 7×24 weekly heatmap + per-month grid
- Details — filterable raw sessions table
- Apps & Categories — aggregated bar lists
